name: Harold, O'Brien
description: Pat is little Pat is small But when it comes to doing Pat is with them all.
text: Pat is little Pat is small But when it comes to doing Pat is with them all.

name: Leo, Bartlett
description: Stiff back, prosperous air, A knowing look and a bushel of care.
text: Stiff back, prosperous air, A knowing look and a bushel of care.

name: Earl, Borchert
description: If you were here as long as I have been You'd feel as rusty as a piece of tin You wouldn't care to move a step No, sir! By gum, you'd be "sans pep."
text: If you were here as long as I have been You'd feel as rusty as a piece of tin You wouldn't care to move a step No, sir! By gum, you'd be "sans pep."

name: Shearer, William
description: Beneath his stiff sombrero He seems quite dignified, But in a cozy corner Oh, boy! "You'd be surprised."
text: Beneath his stiff sombrero He seems quite dignified, But in a cozy corner Oh, boy! "You'd be surprised."

name: Gilligan, Neil
description: Gilligan came from the Eastern Coast. He has done his bit and will never boast.
text: Gilligan came from the Eastern Coast. He has done his bit and will never boast.

name: Benson, Ed
description: Ed the aggie of Ely fame, Hence the word "Hunyock" for a name.
text: Ed the aggie of Ely fame, Hence the word "Hunyock" for a name.

name: Harker, Ernest
description: Fat but faithful, Studious bent, Industrious worker, Capable gent.
text: Fat but faithful, Studious bent, Industrious worker, Capable gent.

name: Avis, Lothrop
description: Avis is a Senior with a smile for everyone, with fair and pretty features and a heart that's full of fun.
text: Avis is a Senior with a smile for everyone, with fair and pretty features and a heart that's full of fun.

name: Catherine, Somers
clubs: Transferred from Los Angeles State Normal
description: Coach of the winning team, a quiet, progressing way that makes the U. of N. long for her to stay.
text: Coach of the winning team, a quiet, progressing way that makes the U. of N. long for her to stay.

name: Julia, Callahan
description: Julia is a Senior fair, a girl with tri-delt grace. A charming disposition that befits her charming face.
text: Julia is a Senior fair, a girl with tri-delt grace. A charming disposition that befits her charming face.

name: Albert, E. Cahlan
description: Small, bright, sure, and fast; By the U. of N. to the very last.
text: Small, bright, sure, and fast; By the U. of N. to the very last.

name: Antonio, Banzon
description: Tony is a regular guy When he gets his fingers in the political pie In the Philippine Islands, south in the sea He'll get good jobs for you and me.
text: Tony is a regular guy When he gets his fingers in the political pie In the Philippine Islands, south in the sea He'll get good jobs for you and me.

name: Joseph, D. Hill
description: Joe has a smile Most all the while He's Jimmy's beau As all must know.
text: Joe has a smile Most all the while He's Jimmy's beau As all must know.

name: Nellie, Mcwilliams
description: From the Southern part of the State, Nellie comes to our dear U. She is always full of smiles and jokes for anyone who is sad or blue.
text: From the Southern part of the State, Nellie comes to our dear U. She is always full of smiles and jokes for anyone who is sad or blue.

name: John, Belford
description: What's the use of working? Any fool can work. But when it comes to shirking, Boy! he sure can shirk.
text: What's the use of working? Any fool can work. But when it comes to shirking, Boy! he sure can shirk.

name: Veva, Campbell Davis
description: The start—the meeting—a kiss—the ring—the time—Davis.
text: The start—the meeting—a kiss—the ring—the time—Davis.

name: Andrew, Aiken
description: Some say that Jock is a queener of note, On all pretty women they say he does dote We've seen him with this one, we've seen him with that, And as for picking, he's right at the bat.
text: Some say that Jock is a queener of note, On all pretty women they say he does dote We've seen him with this one, we've seen him with that, And as for picking, he's right at the bat.

name: Clarence, Rice
description: Clarence Rice came from O. A. C. Now he is our prize Aggie.
text: Clarence Rice came from O. A. C. Now he is our prize Aggie.

name: Thomas, B. Jones
description: Tom is quiet, sure and willing, With success he leaves the U. His deep thoughts never spilling, Loyal to the Silver and Blue.
text: Tom is quiet, sure and willing, With success he leaves the U. His deep thoughts never spilling, Loyal to the Silver and Blue.

name: Jimmie, Odbert
description: A charming senior she Peppy and lively be it said And her name is simply Jimmie And be it known she's some co-ed.
text: A charming senior she Peppy and lively be it said And her name is simply Jimmie And be it known she's some co-ed.

name: Rachel, Sprague
description: Miss Rachel Sprague, a close eye keeps peeled Around the department of the home workery; For she is afraid that someone may steal Something from the cupboards of the cookery.
text: Miss Rachel Sprague, a close eye keeps peeled Around the department of the home workery; For she is afraid that someone may steal Something from the cupboards of the cookery.

name: Adele, Armstrong
description: Blue eyes, blue eyes, We surmise, like sunrise, Sweet smile, honey chile, Just Adele, all the while.
text: Blue eyes, blue eyes, We surmise, like sunrise, Sweet smile, honey chile, Just Adele, all the while.

name: Meyer, Louie
description: Pinochle shark and good at chess, Engineering—Oh, what a mess.
text: Pinochle shark and good at chess, Engineering—Oh, what a mess.

name: Knight, John
description: John Knight Is alright In storm or fair He's always there.
text: John Knight Is alright In storm or fair He's always there.

name: Smith, Morris T.
description: M. T. his initials So M. T. we call him Let him hear you say "girls" And M. T. comes fallin'.
text: M. T. his initials So M. T. we call him Let him hear you say "girls" And M. T. comes fallin'.

name: Dorothy, Churchill
description: Dot, when the college sun has set The trials of life are here; We look back on the good old campus And think of your good cheer.
text: Dot, when the college sun has set The trials of life are here; We look back on the good old campus And think of your good cheer.

name: Mildred, Griswold
description: Why take life so serious Says Millie to herself; 'Tis now we must enjoy it For old age soon makes delirious And casts us on the shelf.
text: Why take life so serious Says Millie to herself; 'Tis now we must enjoy it For old age soon makes delirious And casts us on the shelf.

name: Wright, Lewis
description: Doctor Wright who got his "Doc" At Cornell a'healing stock Is a man of broadened mind, A man of men all the time.
text: Doctor Wright who got his "Doc" At Cornell a'healing stock Is a man of broadened mind, A man of men all the time.

name: Frost, Jack
description: Jack of the Aviation After the call for men, "Frostie" of nineteen twenty May he return again.
text: Jack of the Aviation After the call for men, "Frostie" of nineteen twenty May he return again.

name: Sweetman, Marie
description: Marie is never seen except when in class; Where else could find such a studious lass?
text: Marie is never seen except when in class; Where else could find such a studious lass?

name: Layman, Lawrence
description: In every trick and prank Cupid has played his part; But the worse he ever did Was to shoot at Laurie's heart.
text: In every trick and prank Cupid has played his part; But the worse he ever did Was to shoot at Laurie's heart.

name: Walter, Wallace A.
description: From San Diego comes Vernon's rep And with it also comes his pep, He's called the city's very best, We'll say he sure has stood the test.
text: From San Diego comes Vernon's rep And with it also comes his pep, He's called the city's very best, We'll say he sure has stood the test.

name: Overstrom, George
description: George is a progressive gobbler, A layman's hand has he; He is ever willing and working To keep up his pedigree.
text: George is a progressive gobbler, A layman's hand has he; He is ever willing and working To keep up his pedigree.

name: Hooten, Marian Lombard
description: To Hooten from Lombard, She's always the same, No difference at all If she did change her name.
text: To Hooten from Lombard, She's always the same, No difference at all If she did change her name.

name: Turley, Eleanor
description: On the Campus is a girlie By the name of Eleanor Turley, To beat her at making noise Or gathering frat pins from the boys You sure have to get up early.
text: On the Campus is a girlie By the name of Eleanor Turley, To beat her at making noise Or gathering frat pins from the boys You sure have to get up early.

name: Lee, Scott
description: He used to be bashful but now not at all; Why he even shimmies with his shadow on the wall; By the name of "Scotty" he is known to all; And he is also Mayor of the boys at the Hall.
text: He used to be bashful but now not at all; Why he even shimmies with his shadow on the wall; By the name of "Scotty" he is known to all; And he is also Mayor of the boys at the Hall.

name: Badt, Morris
description: Morris Badt has been the editor Of the '19 Artemisia; We would have you know That with favors Badt can please you.
text: Morris Badt has been the editor Of the '19 Artemisia; We would have you know That with favors Badt can please you.

name: Simon, Merenbach
description: He has old Rip Van Winkle Thrown in the shade; By the stars that twinkle Will he ever learn a trade.
text: He has old Rip Van Winkle Thrown in the shade; By the stars that twinkle Will he ever learn a trade.

name: Paul, Hornaday
description: Paul the Navy ensign Sails for the coast of Fame. His ship with a crab is anchored But he leaves without a stain.
text: Paul the Navy ensign Sails for the coast of Fame. His ship with a crab is anchored But he leaves without a stain.

name: Gooding, Everett
motto:
Life is only a trial
And when it is gone
Nothing lingers but a smile.
text:
Life is only a trial
And when it is gone
Nothing lingers but a smile.

name: Hart, Henry H.
description:
In deeds
He leads;
Indeed we need
Men of his creed.
text:
In deeds
He leads;
Indeed we need
Men of his creed.

name: Malone, George W.
description: Molly Malone had longer for the time When he could smash the Bear Cubs' line The time came round, Molly was there The Cubs couldn't stop him, away he would tear.
text: Molly Malone had longer for the time When he could smash the Bear Cubs' line The time came round, Molly was there The Cubs couldn't stop him, away he would tear.

name: Carlsen, Nels P.
description: You may talk of Nels if you will But he has one of the best co-eds on the Hill.
text: You may talk of Nels if you will But he has one of the best co-eds on the Hill.

name: Olmsted, Kenyon
description: Please don't consider this book a frost Just because Kenyon's picture was lost. He wears a stiff sombrero And is quite modest and shy, He is an excellent student And always a regular guy.
text: Please don't consider this book a frost Just because Kenyon's picture was lost. He wears a stiff sombrero And is quite modest and shy, He is an excellent student And always a regular guy.

name: Philip, Frank
description: Good things in small packages come, And Phil is the smallest of all. As a college leader he makes things hum, And we know that Phil answers his call.
text: Good things in small packages come, And Phil is the smallest of all. As a college leader he makes things hum, And we know that Phil answers his call.

name: Jones, Lester C.
description: A brunette dear, a night of cheer, An early class, Oh, lass—alas!
text: A brunette dear, a night of cheer, An early class, Oh, lass—alas!

name: Eden, Gerry W.
description: Constant to "one" and faithful and true, The biggest student at Nevada U. As Clionia's manager Gerry shines, Here is his record in just three lines.
text: Constant to "one" and faithful and true, The biggest student at Nevada U. As Clionia's manager Gerry shines, Here is his record in just three lines.

name: Reed, Albert J.
description: Al's record in France was crowned with fame, In leaving the U. it is just the same.
text: Al's record in France was crowned with fame, In leaving the U. it is just the same.

name: Underwood, Anne
description: Pep's personified in Ann. Though she has a pious man.
text: Pep's personified in Ann. Though she has a pious man.

name: Olds, Valentine
description: Oh, Valentine To thee we sing; Thou surely art A little thing. And yet so round And husky, too; Oh "Dynamite" We sing to you.
text: Oh, Valentine To thee we sing; Thou surely art A little thing. And yet so round And husky, too; Oh "Dynamite" We sing to you.

name: Hogg, Melita
description: Eyes of blue, That upward smile, Kind of funny little style; Brain, a lot, Good natured, too; She's O. K. although she's new.
text: Eyes of blue, That upward smile, Kind of funny little style; Brain, a lot, Good natured, too; She's O. K. although she's new.

name: Sullivan, Louise
description: Louise is the vamp, Who vies with June In keeping Waite's heart Always out of tune.
text: Louise is the vamp, Who vies with June In keeping Waite's heart Always out of tune.

name: Elliott, M. Mitchell
description: The bottle's gone. Happy the day When old John B. comes back to stay. We'll have some more—so here's to you, And here's goodbye to home-made brew.
text: The bottle's gone. Happy the day When old John B. comes back to stay. We'll have some more—so here's to you, And here's goodbye to home-made brew.

name: Earl, Gilmstedt
description: He loves the cows and chickens, And he loves to study, too, He loves to run the dickens Out of the Class of '22.
text: He loves the cows and chickens, And he loves to study, too, He loves to run the dickens Out of the Class of '22.

name: John, Wall
description: The firelight flickers on the door A noise is heard in the reading hall, Heavy footsteps grated across the floor, Who else could it be but John Wall?
text: The firelight flickers on the door A noise is heard in the reading hall, Heavy footsteps grated across the floor, Who else could it be but John Wall?

name: John, Douglas
description: Lots of brains, a smiling face, Plenty of jazz, at the head of the race, Never crabs or makes any fuss, What more shall we say of John Douglas.
text: Lots of brains, a smiling face, Plenty of jazz, at the head of the race, Never crabs or makes any fuss, What more shall we say of John Douglas.

name: Hawkins, Lulu
description: Lulu is a wonderful artist, She paints with vision and grace; Please don't mistake my meaning She paints pictures, not her face.
text: Lulu is a wonderful artist, She paints with vision and grace; Please don't mistake my meaning She paints pictures, not her face.

name: Higgins, Virginia C.
description: Jinn's one of those rare jewels Who loves to work and study; Always contented, never frets, She's friend with everybody.
text: Jinn's one of those rare jewels Who loves to work and study; Always contented, never frets, She's friend with everybody.

name: Humphreys, Adelaide
description: Addie is a tiny maid, Who's jolly and sweet; And as for being jazzy, She's always on her feet.
text: Addie is a tiny maid, Who's jolly and sweet; And as for being jazzy, She's always on her feet.

name: Dallas, Vera
description: Where the gray sagebrush spread their pungent odor miles and miles, So Vera, just from California, shows her winning smiles.
text: Where the gray sagebrush spread their pungent odor miles and miles, So Vera, just from California, shows her winning smiles.

name: William L. Carter
description: Long, tall, studious withal; A bachelor of fame, Solo's his game. Women vamps, cold glances get, Bill might fall, but not just yet.
text: Long, tall, studious withal; A bachelor of fame, Solo's his game. Women vamps, cold glances get, Bill might fall, but not just yet.

name: Gavin Yater
description: Mary had a little lad, Smoking was his fad; She took him to the train one day, Result—a letter every day.
text: Mary had a little lad, Smoking was his fad; She took him to the train one day, Result—a letter every day.

name: Rolf E. Brown
description: I've just got time to press my suit, Fix my hair and tie enroute. For at eight you know I have a date, I sure must hurry or I'll be late.
text: I've just got time to press my suit, Fix my hair and tie enroute. For at eight you know I have a date, I sure must hurry or I'll be late.

name: Robert B. Griffith
description: As a yell leader Bob is true, Of the Junior class he's president, too. He's always jazzy and full of fun, And belongs to the class of '21.
text: As a yell leader Bob is true, Of the Junior class he's president, too. He's always jazzy and full of fun, And belongs to the class of '21.

name: Helen, Fuss
description: Helen Fuss's a forward on the girls' basketball team; she has auburn hair and blue eyes, and as a student is very wise.
text: Helen Fuss's a forward on the girls' basketball team; she has auburn hair and blue eyes, and as a student is very wise.

name: Lois, Smythe
description: Last summer she went to Asilomar. There she rested in a tent, where the sand-hills roll. She is now at home doing Calc on a scroll.
text: Last summer she went to Asilomar. There she rested in a tent, where the sand-hills roll. She is now at home doing Calc on a scroll.

name: Hallie, Organ
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Cabinet Secretary (3)
description: Hallie, basketball star, shines everywhere; her rays fall near and far and drive away dull care.
text: Hallie, basketball star, shines everywhere; her rays fall near and far and drive away dull care.

name: Alice, Wall
description: Her smiles are rare and words are few; she belongs to the Y. W. Cabinet and sings in the Glee Club, too.
text: Her smiles are rare and words are few; she belongs to the Y. W. Cabinet and sings in the Glee Club, too.

name: Wooster, Earl
description: Oh, wooing Wooster, of U. N. Can't you let the co-eds be, Or have they vamped you with their ways And kept you in captivity?
text: Oh, wooing Wooster, of U. N. Can't you let the co-eds be, Or have they vamped you with their ways And kept you in captivity?

name: Egan, George
description: "Six o'clock" is thin and tall, He likes to shine in everything, And know ye now, once for all He sure can throw the basketball.
text: "Six o'clock" is thin and tall, He likes to shine in everything, And know ye now, once for all He sure can throw the basketball.

name: Decker, Perl A.
description: Decker has blonde hair that charms most any girl, We all maintain that he deserves the jewel name of Perl.
text: Decker has blonde hair that charms most any girl, We all maintain that he deserves the jewel name of Perl.

name: Wright, Alice Collins
description: Alice is married, there is no more to tell, Her husband cares for her exceedingly well.
text: Alice is married, there is no more to tell, Her husband cares for her exceedingly well.

name: Crump, Ester
description: Ester from Oregon came Just a few years ago. We know she likes Nevada For we never hear her complain.
text: Ester from Oregon came Just a few years ago. We know she likes Nevada For we never hear her complain.

name: Hillhouse, Alethea
description: The fair Alethea once had a man, Brave Gooding of the Phi Sig clan, The man he leaves, Alethea grieves, But to the rescue came Graham.
text: The fair Alethea once had a man, Brave Gooding of the Phi Sig clan, The man he leaves, Alethea grieves, But to the rescue came Graham.

name: Beemer, Mary
description: About Mary there's little to tell, She's good in her studies And rings the class bell.
text: About Mary there's little to tell, She's good in her studies And rings the class bell.

name: Thomas, Buckman
description: At the wheel from the first to last, Can always look back at a loyal past.
text: At the wheel from the first to last, Can always look back at a loyal past.

name: Quilici, Hugo M.
motto: "I so hate all my classes And yet I know I'd die, If during the time that passes I could not have her nigh."
text: "I so hate all my classes And yet I know I'd die, If during the time that passes I could not have her nigh."

name: Caffery, William
description: A big brown car, a jolly smile Is enough to say about Bill—for a while.
text: A big brown car, a jolly smile Is enough to say about Bill—for a while.

name: Boardman, Russell
description: He may seem innocent and quite alright, But back in Chicago he has a girl out of sight. If he makes a stake on a Nevada claim, He'll go back to Chicago and marry his dame.
text: He may seem innocent and quite alright, But back in Chicago he has a girl out of sight. If he makes a stake on a Nevada claim, He'll go back to Chicago and marry his dame.

name: Burke, Emily
description: At the U. of N. we all meet, And meeting strive to do, And Emily does her share and more In making merry the U.
text: At the U. of N. we all meet, And meeting strive to do, And Emily does her share and more In making merry the U.

name: Barnes, Margaret
clubs: Editor Y. W. C. A. Edition Sagebrush (3)
description: Merry, smart and winsome And always ready with a smile; She's industrious as they make them, I think this tells her history in style.
text: Merry, smart and winsome And always ready with a smile; She's industrious as they make them, I think this tells her history in style.

name: Badger, Enola
description: You came from the U. of C. You were a queen in the play, And if you should go, "Woos" would mourn you so, Please don't go away—we pray.
text: You came from the U. of C. You were a queen in the play, And if you should go, "Woos" would mourn you so, Please don't go away—we pray.

name: Shields, Lavina
description: Oh lady fair, He loves your hair, And loves your eyes of blue, Do tell Lavina, is Jimmie true?
text: Oh lady fair, He loves your hair, And loves your eyes of blue, Do tell Lavina, is Jimmie true?

name: John, Philbin
description: From far away England there came an Army Captain with four years of fighting fame. With his Caruso voice and winning way, he makes a host of new friends every day.
text: From far away England there came an Army Captain with four years of fighting fame. With his Caruso voice and winning way, he makes a host of new friends every day.

name: Donald, Hancock
motto: "Why worry" says "Shorty," when you can "bull" your way through. "Do others" is my motto, "before they do you."
text: "Why worry" says "Shorty," when you can "bull" your way through. "Do others" is my motto, "before they do you."

name: John, Gottardi
description: Just as quiet and easy going as he was short years ago, he sure will get a going when he's someone's little beau.
text: Just as quiet and easy going as he was short years ago, he sure will get a going when he's someone's little beau.

name: William, Dewey Conrad
description: Dewey Conrad of Elko fame tried to learn the solo game. So every day, right after glow, the gang does try to teach him how.
text: Dewey Conrad of Elko fame tried to learn the solo game. So every day, right after glow, the gang does try to teach him how.

name: Wogan, Helen
description: Dream on, oh thou dreamer of stories, Golden haired Helen—forget All the trials of the living And you'll be a genius yet.
text: Dream on, oh thou dreamer of stories, Golden haired Helen—forget All the trials of the living And you'll be a genius yet.

name: William, Martin
description: Cap'n Bill Martin of our championship team, Turned all the boys with envy green, For after we licked the Olympic whizzes, He received for a reward the sweetest of kisses.
text: Cap'n Bill Martin of our championship team, Turned all the boys with envy green, For after we licked the Olympic whizzes, He received for a reward the sweetest of kisses.

name: James, J. Bradshaw
description: You can talk about an express train, When it speeds across the plains, But it can't compete with "Rabbit" When he plays his record games.
text: You can talk about an express train, When it speeds across the plains, But it can't compete with "Rabbit" When he plays his record games.

name: Harvey, Luce
description: Harvey is a happy youth And with all he makes good friends, Nor does he with his books and pens Seem to be at all uncouth.
text: Harvey is a happy youth And with all he makes good friends, Nor does he with his books and pens Seem to be at all uncouth.

name: Don, Warren
description: Old Don Warren, king of steworum Editor of the Artemisia, too. With these jobs on his hands, the women he bans, I don't blame him a bit, do you?
text: Old Don Warren, king of steworum Editor of the Artemisia, too. With these jobs on his hands, the women he bans, I don't blame him a bit, do you?

name: Reed, Edward C.
description: Ed is quitting farmer's toil Engineering to pursue, He is weary of the soil And the cattle's noisy "moo."
text: Ed is quitting farmer's toil Engineering to pursue, He is weary of the soil And the cattle's noisy "moo."

name: Chatfield, Charles M.
description: In his work or studies Charles is not sloven And when he plays the piano, it's "this way Beethoven."
text: In his work or studies Charles is not sloven And when he plays the piano, it's "this way Beethoven."

name: Bailey, Gilbert
description: From Colorado to the Sagebrush state, West I went to Nevada U. Where everyone honors the Silver and Blue.
text: From Colorado to the Sagebrush state, West I went to Nevada U. Where everyone honors the Silver and Blue.

name: Waite, Noble
description: Noble the pride of Bunkerville. Short and broad and full of vim, Always ready for a lark, A basketball and Aggie shark.
text: Noble the pride of Bunkerville. Short and broad and full of vim, Always ready for a lark, A basketball and Aggie shark.
